sutras.text: Jordan's promotion pursuit is driven by self-worth anxiety rather than genuine career desire - he hasn't completed his director case draft in months while consistently questioning what he'd think of himself without the title.
Jordan excels at delivery but struggles with leadership (per performance reviews), yet continues pursuing director track without addressing this core gap through concrete skill development.

Jordan's sleep issues (averaging 5.8 hours vs 7-hour goal) directly correlate with relationship distance - late-night phone checking creates physical barrier with Alex and signals emotional unavailability.
Work stress manifests as early morning wake-ups before alarms, creating a cascade that ruins Jordan's presence with Alex and perpetuates the cycle of relationship neglect.
Jordan achieved highest monthly savings when feeling work pride, yet remains paralyzed on major financial decisions - house budget started but incomplete, moving conversations with Alex unfinished.
Paying $2,100 rent monthly while saving for $30k house down payment, but avoiding the actual decision conversation with Alex represents financial discipline without life progression.
Jordan skips gym specifically after long work calls (not just any tiredness), indicating work stress triggers avoidance behaviors that compound physical and mental health decline.

Jordan hasn't asked Alex about their job in two weeks, started but abandoned conversation about moving, and consistently checks phone before bed - pattern of emotional withdrawal disguised as work dedication.
Anniversary planning started but incomplete while therapy sessions continue at $200 each - investing in fixing himself rather than nurturing the relationship that needs attention.
Notion OKR dashboard mentioned across multiple entries but never completed, resume updated repeatedly but never shared - Jordan creates productivity projects instead of addressing core issues.
Working from Epoch Coffee for focus while experiencing back-to-back meetings with no lunch breaks reveals environmental band-aids for systemic work-life boundary problems.
Jordan recognizes burnout symptoms and reads articles about PM burnout, invests heavily in therapy ($200/month), yet continues the same work patterns that created the burnout.

top_insights: Jordan's promotion pursuit is anxiety-driven self-worth seeking, not genuine career growth - address the identity crisis before chasing titles
Sleep issues are the physical manifestation of relationship neglect - the 5.8-hour average directly correlates with emotional distance from Alex
High financial discipline coexists with life decision paralysis - Jordan can save money but can't commit to the future with Alex
unfinished_business: Director promotion case draft (started months ago, never completed)
Conversation with Alex about moving in together and timeline
Anniversary celebration planning (ideas noted, no execution)
Personal OKR dashboard in Notion (mentioned repeatedly, never finished)
Resume updates (repeatedly started, never shared with anyone)
House purchase budget planning (spreadsheet started, decision avoided)
data_story: Jordan is a high-performing product manager caught between ambition and identity crisis, using work intensity to avoid deeper relationship and life decisions. Despite strong financial discipline and mental health investment, he remains stuck in productivity theater - starting systems and plans but never completing them, while his relationship with Alex slowly deteriorates through benign neglect disguised as professional dedication.
